meaning: n.奢侈,铺张,过度,放纵的言行
examples: Going to the theatre is our only extravagance.
Sponging and taking more than one's share and extravagance and waste on the part of cadres must be banned.
When the company went under, tales of his extravagance surged through the industry.
ECB's extravagance sparked fears that it knew about a nasty problem in European banking.
With the passing years, Fellini approached his material with an extravagance that bordered on the bombastic.
